meta.title: The World-Model Program — Mega Doc
meta.description: Substrate, convergence, sim engines, the distinguishability spine, and the hardened build program — one page.

props.title: Compute That Scales With Surprise
props.subtitle: An agent that only thinks when the world changes in a way that matters — and seals a replayable receipt for every decision, including the ones it skipped.
props.meta: Mega doc · built + prior-art mapped + design hardened · 2026-06-12

props.content: Three independent research lineages converged on one mechanism. The substrate is built and green on main, the comparison is mapped with file-level evidence, and the next-generation design survived seven rounds of adversarial review. This page is the whole story.

props.label: tests green on main (62 reconciler + 111 carrier)
gates — one identity relation at three cost tiers
build slices, every one names its deletion

props.content:
Most AI agents are *busy* — they re-poll, re-embed, and re-reason on a clock whether or not anything changed. This substrate inverts that. Every observation passes three gates in cost-ascending order: **is it legal? → did anything material change? → is it surprising enough to act on?** A skip at any gate is nearly free. Passing all three triggers work and seals a content-addressed receipt that supersedes its predecessor.

The receipt chain replays **without keys**: identical input yields a byte-identical decision regardless of which prediction model was installed. You can prove what the system did — and prove what it deliberately did not do. The surprise judge is a swappable world-model behind a one-number port that the loop never trusts: a missing, stale, or garbage readout forces a render, never a silent skip.
The Constraint Manifold decides what may run. The World-Model decides what is worth running — and proves it.

props.label: MECHANISM
props.title: Three gates, cheapest first
props.subtitle: One observation-indexed identity relation, walked coarse-to-fine. A skip short-circuits everything after it.

props.title: G1 · Admissible?
props.content: Structural legality — indistinguishability under the structural measurement set. **Free.**

props.title: G2 · Material?
props.content: Canonicalize → SHA-256. The degenerate single-probe case: unchanged hash ⇒ skip. **Cheap.**

props.title: G3 · Surprising?
props.content: Graded distinguishability — the predictor returns one non-negative scalar (von-Neumann relative entropy); render past threshold. **Expensive.**

props.title: → Render + Receipt
props.content: Patch beliefs, seal a content-addressed receipt. Keyless replay forever: same input, byte-identical decision, predictor-independent.

props.label: CONVERGENCE
props.title: Three lineages, one mechanism
props.subtitle: Independently derived, then compared with file-level evidence (12 analyst agents, 2 reports).

props.title: 🧬 The QIT line — Joshua Eisenhart
props.content: Codex-Ratchet (v4 → v5 QIT engine) + the FEP-to-quantum-information thesis + the levos_v10 Lev-native packet. Source of G3's *semantics*: surprise as relative entropy, the attractor-basin ontology, observation-indexed equality `Eq_ctx ⟺ Ind_ctx`, and the three sim engines. One author, one evolving system — his docs designate Lev as host.

props.title: 🏗️ The lev-internal line
props.content: Keyless-replay was demanded by WM-8 acceptance criteria two months before any external intake. Receipt hash-chains, admission gates, the surprise-threshold topology, append-only LevEvents with actor + time — all in-house. The three-gate cost-ascending stack and the pluggable scalar port have no ancestor in any studied source.

props.title: ⚙️ The memo-skip confirmation
props.content: An external reactive harness intook at verdict extract/P0 confirmed the economics: canonicalize-then-fingerprint memo keys, skip-unchanged, cost scales with genuine novelty. Honest verdict after comparison: one mechanism crystallized plus a tagline — nearly everything else was already in-house or convergent.

items.title: Codex-Ratchet v4 intake — the distinguishability formal spike
items.content: Probe-relative equality, exclusion vocabulary, fail-closed guards enter lev.

items.title: The founding day
items.content: Josh: "FEP-based simulation model… pure QIT." Same day: the world-model stack design, the 7-agent research sweep, and the pivot — build it ourselves, zero code dependency.

items.title: Two root constraints + attractor basin
items.content: Josh's constraint captures fork two descendants: the formal-methods POC (demotes QIT, proves admission/replay in Z3+TLA+) and the world-model lane (adopts QIT as the belief carrier).

items.title: Codex-Ratchet v5 — the QIT engine manifold
items.content: Distinguishability full math, Lindblad dynamics, basin admission ladder, monostability result. Self-frozen under audit one day before the final push.

items.title: Convergence week
items.content: Memo-skip mechanics confirmed by external intake; reconciler spine lands; first-principles re-derivation; 6-round critic loop; 9-slice hardening build, first-pass green.

items.title: levos_v10 packet arrives
items.content: Josh's Lev-native upgrade spec: the three sim engines, observation-indexed equality, contamination gates, eight lev.* schemas.

items.title: Merged · mapped · re-designed
items.content: Everything on main. Two prior-art reports pushed. The distinguishability-unification design + proposal survive 7 adversarial rounds.

items.title: The build program — 9 slices, 4 waves
items.content: Theorem tests, eval hard cutover, composition root (PM-1 flips green), sim-eval lanes, control families, basin honesty gate.

props.label: THE PAYLOAD
props.title: The three sim engines — JAX, Julia, PyTorch
props.subtitle: The author's own headline: evaluator provider lanes behind Exec — never runtime owners, never in the hot path.

rows: Semantic authority — finite algebra, basin/global-stability analysis, interval & reachability certificates, proof obligations
High-throughput differentiable — batched ODE/SDE solves, counterexample search, vectorized basin approximation
Graph / geometric / learned — message passing over graph projections, receipt-lineage anomaly scoring
A learned surprise predictor over the receipt chain
Cheap deterministic runtime guards — the ONLY hot-path lane

props.content: Every candidate library runs one pipeline before it can matter: **support probe → bounded micro-eval → typed observation → code-owned scorer fact → proof reference → policy decision.** A passing fixture is not admission; counterexamples stay in the lineage even after later success. Tensor exchange is transport-only — raw NumPy, pickle, and adjacency-as-truth are forbidden as canonical state. The packet ships a buildable plugin skeleton; truth stays decided in exactly one place.

props.label: THE SPINE
props.title: When are two world-states the same?
props.subtitle: The question all three lineages answer — at four altitudes. The founding axiom: identity requires distinguishability.

rows: Equal iff no probe in the declared family distinguishes them — s₁ ~ s₂ ⟺ ∀m∈M : m(s₁)=m(s₂)
Eq_ctx ⟺ Ind_ctx merge rule (planned, gates merges only)
Relative entropy below threshold — graded distinguishability, one scalar
Trace distance with the Helstrom guessing bound; the Hopf fiber as the canonical byte-different-but-identical state

props.content: The witness-based equality is **strictly more rigorous than hash equality as an equality theory** — byte-different states can be one world-state (the Hopf fiber proves it). But the two answer different questions: hash equality decides *skip recompute?* where over-distinguishing is safe; witness equality decides *merge two world-objects?* where over-identifying is catastrophic. The design keeps both, each scoped to its question — and the three gates turn out to be **one quotient construction walked coarse-to-fine.** That unification is the load-bearing idea of the next iteration.

props.title: ✅ Genuinely ours
props.content: The three-gate cost-ascending topology with the proven G2-is-zero-surprise-floor theorem. The pluggable scalar predictor port with fail-closed provenance. Predictor-independent byte-identical replay as a *tested property*. Receipts as LevEvents (actor + time + effect id) with supersede compare-and-swap. The co-equal-representations ontology ruling — no privileged root.

props.title: 📥 Extracted (credited at release)
props.content: G3's semantic content — the FEP→QIT swap, surprise as relative entropy, basin vocabulary, two root constraints (Josh). The canonicalize→fingerprint memo-skip discipline and the cost-scales-with-surprise tagline (the reactive-harness intake). Admissibility culture and exclusion vocabulary (Codex-Ratchet v4).

props.title: 🔀 Convergent siblings
props.content: The formal-methods POC stated the admission predicate and replay semantics in Z3/TLA+ with zero awareness of the reconciler. An in-repo pipeline built receipt hash-chains independently, pre-intake. The keyless-replay *requirement* predates the external mechanism by two months. The pattern was in the air; the evidence says so.

props.label: NEGATIVE KNOWLEDGE
props.title: What the v5 engine teaches us not to claim
props.subtitle: Imported now so no future session re-fights it.

props.title: Monostability
props.content: 200/200 random states converged to ONE fixed point under a fixed map — the apparent 4-class structure was schedule memory, not basins. Binding rule: no basin-occupancy claims from fixed engines. Canon says **candidate_basin** until a second representation passes the spike.

props.title: Control families or it didn't happen
props.content: No scalar counts without nulls: terrain-erased, time-reversed, random matched-norm. The origin system's own bridge fails this bar — which is exactly why it is the bar. The G3 wiring slice imports it as an admissibility condition.

props.title: Frozen under audit, and that's the import
props.content: The v5 engine self-froze over operator-axis provenance drift — 26 scouts quarantined, admission disallowed, one final push. We are reading a deliberately frozen state, not a finished engine. The freeze discipline itself is the thing worth porting.

props.label: HARDENING
props.title: The design loop that produced the next iteration

props.content:
Twenty interview questions, each answered by an architect agent with **one committed position** — no option menus — grounded in file-line evidence with rejected alternatives recorded. The synthesized design carries a decision register (every item under 5% ambiguity), a premortem, jobs-to-be-done, a reverse brainstorm, figure-storming from domain experts and named engineers/scientists, a vocabulary firewall, and a **deletion ledger** — because hard cutover means every slice deletes something.

Then the adversarial loop: a read-only architect critic charged to *predict footguns, find all gotchas, and make the plan foolproof*, returning evidence-backed verdicts; a patcher instructed to strengthen gates, never weaken them to pass review.

rows.text: final major hand-fixed with the critic's verbatim remedy

props.label: NEXT
props.title: The build program — 9 slices, 4 waves, every slice deletes something

rows: Pin the one-relation-three-tiers construction as runnable property tests on the existing gate seams
One EvalVerdict mint, attestation/score-report split, single derivation function
The fused scorecard type, a duplicate verdict mint, two private decider bodies
Bracketed composition with law profiles — measured 78% correlation destruction under reordering says no canonicalizer may assume commutativity
Wire the carrier predictor into G3 behind the control-family bar — the expected-fail gate flips green
The fixed seed-42 baseline prior; the permanent xfail
Provider lanes behind effect dispatch, truth decided in eval only, copy-then-modify from the shipped skeleton
Any path for solvers into the hot loop
Real looked-up priors; stale evidence becomes an evaluation error, never a default
Scalar admissibility requires beating its nulls
Canon claims candidate_basin until the two-representation spike passes; verdict vocabulary from the origin's admission ladder
Unproven basin language in product claims
The separability bridge-leak guard mechanically tests the correlated-facts-are-entangled invariant

props.label: OPEN
props.title: Four decisions on the owner's desk
1 · Build entry
props.content: Wave 1 as planned (three independent parallel lanes) vs keystone-first (composition root — smallest diff, biggest honesty gain) vs park.

props.title: 2 · First sim lane
props.content: JAX fits the two nearest numeric needs (stress sweep + representation spike). PyTorch is where the origin author's depth is. Julia is the proof-grade lane. Or defer until a slice needs numerics.

props.title: 3 · Basin honesty downgrade
props.content: Apply candidate_basin language to product docs now, hedge with a footnote, or leave until the spike verdict.

props.title: 4 · The entanglement claim
props.content: The origin's own correlated-information signal turned out to be sampling noise. Hedge our 'correlated facts are entangled' claim now, keep it until the falsifier runs, or drop it from product language.

props.label: THROUGHLINE
props.title: Intelligence that is accountable to reality

props.content:
Today's agents *generate*. This substrate lets them **believe, prove, share, model, and seek** — with receipts. The feature ladder compounds from here: fork-and-replay counterfactual histories, many agents reconciling one content-addressed mind, falsifiable models of people and domains, and finally agents that act to minimize their own expected surprise — curiosity as a control law.

The difference between this and a demo is the discipline: claims carry their own kill criteria, scalars carry their nulls, beliefs carry receipts, and every migration deletes what it replaces.
